source: Ainsworth et al. 2011 Compendium [24]; Strath et al. 2013 Med Sci Sports Exerc
justification: Between-study variability in MET-based EE estimates across moderate-intensity exercise modalities. SD=0.30 reflects ~7% CV around midpoint of moderate range. Strath et al. report SEM of MET estimates ~0.3-0.5 across methods. Provisional.
Within-study SD of individual MET values. Uncertainty in this SD itself is SD=0.08. Provisional.

source: Avila et al. 2023 systematic review of exercise adherence in weight-loss RCTs; STRRIDE [19,20]
justification: Mean adherence across supervised exercise trials ranges 0.72-0.92. SD=0.06 reflects between-trial variability. Specific between-study variance not formally estimated from meta-analysis.

Penalty estimated from STRRIDE dose-adherence differential. Only one programme provides direct evidence. SD=0.015 is author estimate of uncertainty. STRRIDE is also used in validation sensitivity — partial circularity.

Frankenfield reports ~82% non-obese and ~75% obese adults predicted within 10% of measured RMR. SD=0.02 reflects uncertainty in this error estimate across populations. Full between-individual distribution not formally estimated.
